figures.caption: Figure 1. In the conference room scene, the upper part of the figure shows that the sparse point cloud causes the foreground points and background points to overlap, while the lower part shows that in the Gaussian space, the background is completely blocked by the foreground.

Figure 3. Qualitative comparison of unsupervised segmentation on the S3DIS validation set. Each color represents one semantic class. For better understanding, we show some of the color and name matches at the bottom.
Figure 4. Qualitative comparison with Ground Truth of unsupervised segmentation on the ScanNet validation set. Each color represents one semantic class.

Figure 2. The pipeline of our method. Given an indoor point cloud, we first generate multi-view projections and apply Gaussian splatting. Then, the rendered images are segmented by SAM, and semantic cues are transferred back to 3D Gaussians. Finally, the segmented Gaussians are refined and aligned with the raw point cloud through the alignment module of Gaussians and points, and labels are propagated back to the original points.
Figure 5. Qualitative comparison of different ablation settings on the ScanNet validation set.
